author: @marcfruchtman9473
text:
Inventiveness is definitely an iterative process but "Marketing" for a local business is a completely different animal. If he doesn't have "visibility" by pedestrian or vehicle traffic then he needs to consider signage that visible shows the prepared dishes and points to the restaurant. 
Honestly, I don't think that youtube is a good choice for a "local" restaurant's advertising energy...

author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text: Most people like to chat , for various reasons and it is a psychology science domain . But the general advice "just do it" is totally wrong. Not in this instance (because making YouTube videos as commercial , as a small business owner doesn't cost anything and the worst thing that can happen is , that very few people see those videos). However , you are a successful Youtuber , with thousands videos uploaded , so that neighbor came to a right place for advice , even though you have more ideas and projects than time to do it all , so no time to waste on chatting .  However , I saw a lot of situations when people "just did it" and regretted soon after , because they didn't bother to talk things trough (chatted with people , who were aware of potential problems  and had overall comprehension of situation). Those were experts in their field , with lots of experience (no fools , by any means) , but the arrogance of that experience and acquired knowledge costed them a lot . In my mind , never hurts to talk with people about ideas , especially if they done stuff like that before. Chatting , as a pass time activity , is another matter .

author: @russellzauner
text:
Do it while you can.
I am still trying to get a workspace together even though the accident was way back in 2017 - if you are doing things on your own, it's even more of a setback when unexpected things happen.
Now, I did have a pretty full first 50 years of life - my accident was after a 4 day weekend spent at a huge family campout on a river where we're rafting every day and playing bocce (it was the Italian side); took a bad step off the stairs at the restaurant on the way home and ended up crippled, lost everything I had earned and saved, lost my house, common story in the US.  I was working for Intel for about 15 years at that point and one of the top eng techs in Oregon, on vacation when it happened.
Do it while you can.  You may not get even the chance that I did to try and regain some ability to do it on your own; do it and do it as soon as you can.

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: i have a quote I came up with "we didn't create language, language created us." Language is itself a tool we use, and we do talk, don't we? I wanted to do a dissertation on this fact because some French linguist (Tabouret-Keller) had said animals cannot communicate - perhaps she meant to say communicate abstracted intellectual concepts. [ Edit an hour later or so: by abstracted intellectual concepts I mean creating new relational meaning between semiotic signifiers to convolute basic emoted syntax into semantic complexes, which in this case are articulated words, and not merely emoted intentions, which is my view on how animals can in fact and do communicate, in quite complex ways, that are not defined linguistically, however - hence their behavior]. Language is a utility to convey meaning in regards constructive cooperation, and we built language to do this, I also think this is why language developed, as (not only) a parallel to human technological development, even from more rudimentary technologies like the mighty flint and tinder. Without articulated terminologies, could we emote like lions growl and emote their intentions with bodily gestures, to go on a hunt, or to subdue a competitive male? No we need these relational articulated constructs (words), while animals can get by with emosion. However we also rely on these emosions to emote meaning, it is just that science cannot be emoted, although I'm sure some birds when building nests can do a bit of cheerleading and chirp excitedly to get the others to become enthused. i don't think cheerleading is a human-only activity! This reminds me of Dr. Franz Mesmer (1734-1815) who had his medical license revoked for his theory on "Animal Magnetism" a concept which today we know as Mesmerism. Perhaps you could do a video about animal magnetism, sir Robert sir? That would be highly entertaining! hahaha! On a more serious note I do think Dr. Mesmer was onto something, as there is some type of gravity of being that cause people to become attracted to others - measuring such events scientifically remains beyond the scope of this discussion!  https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Franz_Mesmer

author: @ibme8359
text: Are we talking about it or (trading ideas), there is a big DIFFERENCE. You do talk allot about many things (I consider that you sharing your ideas)! I myself glean ideas, and occasionally parse (talk) them by a friend. Next I find a pen and paper and (do something), make a rough draft and set about collecting materials for the job. Talk, muse about it, gather some stuff and commence the job! "Failing to plan is planning to fail"! IMO talking is part of planning!!!

author: @hunnybunnysheavymetalmusic6542
text:
If I don't talk a thing over, I never do it.

We're all different though.

Also, since I lack any money, I have to wait until I have money to do anything, and if I have not embedded it into my mental banks, they eventually evaporate.

I spend a lot of time looking and observing, lightly tinkering, and then talking thing through before I can feel confident to do most of what I am trying to do.

This also helps me to find cheaper, easier, simpler solutions.
